body.title: Ground Loop proxy masks backend 4xx/5xx as 502
body.description:
devices/ground_loop/plugin_proxy.py forwards with urllib.request.urlopen, which raises HTTPError on any 4xx/5xx. The except clause turns ALL of them into send_error(502), so a legitimate backend 404/401/500 reaches the client as 502. Observed: /api/status, /healthz, /api/feed return 502 through :8078 (some are genuinely absent/disabled routes, but the client can't tell 404 from a real proxy failure). Fix: catch urllib.error.HTTPError separately and pass its real status + body through; reserve 502 for actual connection/transport failures (URLError).

**Affected files:** devices/ground_loop/plugin_proxy.py
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** IN: status-code fidelity in _forward. OUT: CONNECT tunnel, backend spawn logic.
**Test plan:** add a test hitting a known-404 backend route through the proxy and asserting 404 (not 502); add one asserting a real connection-refused yields 502.
body.result: Fixed in e80f0feb: _forward catches HTTPError separately and passes backend 4xx/5xx through verbatim; 502 reserved for transport failures. 2 regression tests (404 passthrough, conn-refused→502) pass. View-tool akien label also corrected to 'yours' (not legacy).
